title: Goal Coupling

content.question: What are you doing that doesn't actually serve your core mission?
sop: Write your core mission in one clear sentence.
List every current project, habit, or commitment you're actively spending time on.
Rate each one's Relevance to the main goal, 1-10 (see calculation, page 5).
Sum the relevance scores to see how coupled your actual activity is to your stated mission.
Drop or renegotiate anything scoring below a 4, it's consuming resources without advancing the mission.
questions: What are you doing that doesn't actually serve your core mission?
If you wrote your actual mission down and checked your calendar against it, how much of this week would survive?
Which project are you keeping alive out of momentum rather than because it still fits where you're going?
content.equation_example: Rate each active project's Relevance to your main goal, 1-10, then sum them. Project A 8 plus Project B 3 plus Project C 6 equals Alignment score 17 out of a possible 30 (3 projects times 10). Higher means most of your effort is actually pointed at your stated goal; lower means you're busy in directions that don't add up to it.
content.closing_statement: Every commitment should trace back to one destination.

caption:
Principle Nº 200: Goal Coupling

Sub-goals align under higher purpose.

Ask yourself: What are you doing that doesn't actually serve your core mission?
